headline: Who’s funding the District 1 race?
dek: Donor data for the District 1 council race, comparing Jon Kaji and David Kartsonis.

geo.heading: Where do the donors live?
geo.sub: 31% of Kaji’s donors are Torrance residents. 76% of Kartsonis’s are.

stats.heading: Donors at the legal maximum
stats.sub: Torrance caps individual donations at $1,000 per election. Percentage of each candidate’s maxed-out donors who are from outside Torrance:

cards.label: Kaji’s maxed-out donors from outside Torrance
Kartsonis’s maxed-out donors from outside Torrance

occupation.heading: Where do the donor dollars come from?
occupation.sub: Each candidate’s donor dollars grouped by occupation as reported on their filings.<sup>1</sup>

contrast.heading: Occupation comparison
contrast.sub: The two occupation groups from the chart above shown side by side: business owners, professionals, and their family<sup>2</sup> vs. teachers, public servants, and service workers.

labels:
Business owners, professionals,

callouts.title: Real estate industry donors
callouts.body: Kaji received contributions from seven real estate and property professionals (25% of donor dollars), including Tommy Murakoshi, who listed Kaji & Associates as his employer, and Aurelio Mattucci, who is also running for City Treasurer. Kartsonis received contributions from two property professionals (14% of donor dollars).

callouts.title: Shared donors across slate
callouts.body: Mattucci donated to both Kaji and George Chen’s mayoral campaign. For comparison, see Chen’s donor geography.

callouts.title: Out-of-state donors
callouts.body: Kaji received contributions from donors in Hawaii, Colorado, Washington, and Texas. Kartsonis’s filings show none from outside California.

callouts.title: Average donation by geography
callouts.body: Kaji’s average donation: $347 from Torrance-based donors, $588 from non-Torrance donors (non-Torrance 69% higher). Kartsonis’s average donation: $363 from Torrance-based donors, $308 from non-Torrance donors (Torrance 15% higher).

footnotes.body: “Business owners, professionals, and their family” includes donors with filed titles of: business owner, president, CEO, CFO, founder, executive, engineer, programmer, physician, doctor, attorney, real estate broker/agent/developer, property investor, financial planner, finance manager, CPA, architect, director, manager, consultant, pharmacist, banker, insurance agent, and accountant — as well as family members (see note 2). “Teachers, public servants, and service workers” includes: teacher, professor, librarian, police officer, mayor, mechanic, bank teller, pastoral assistant, sales, instructor, and similar titles. “Retired” and “not employed” are reported as filed.

“Their family” identifies donors who (a) filed a job title of unemployed, housewife, or homemaker and (b) share a last name with at least one other donor to the same candidate. Shared last names do not prove a family relationship — common surnames may produce false matches. This grouping surfaces a pattern, not an assertion of specific relationships.
All data from $100+ contributions filed with the City of Torrance through December 31, 2025 via Torrance NetFile. Contributions under $100 are not required to be itemized and are not included.
